skill.name: firecrawl-agent
skill.description: AI-powered autonomous data extraction that navigates complex sites and returns structured JSON. Use this skill when the user wants structured data from websites, needs to extract pricing tiers, product listings, directory entries, or any data as JSON with a schema.
skill.summary: AI web scraping agent that autonomously navigates websites and extracts structured data as JSON.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 19 patterns but all are false positives. The flagged 'external_commands' are documentation examples in SKILL.md showing CLI usage, not actual code execution. Path traversal references are documentation links to sibling skill files. Weak crypto flags hit on YAML frontmatter. The skill uses properly scoped allowed-tools (Bash firecrawl * and npx firecrawl *) with explicit command allowlisting. Network access is inherent to web scraping functionality and is legitimate.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Skill executes firecrawl CLI commands via Bash. Commands are allowlisted with wildcards (firecrawl *, npx firecrawl *) which provides scope control but could be expanded by installing malicious firecrawl package versions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: allowed-tools declares Bash execution with wildcard patterns - legitimate for CLI tool but trust depends on firecrawl package integrity

content.user_title: Extract Structured Data from Websites Autonomously
content.value_statement: Manual web scraping across multiple pages is time-consuming and requires technical skills. This AI agent autonomously navigates complex websites and extracts structured data as JSON without writing custom scrapers.
actual_capabilities: Autonomously navigate multi-page websites to find and extract data
Extract structured data matching a provided JSON schema
Identify and extract pricing tiers, product listings, and directory entries
Output extracted data as JSON files or inline results
Focus extraction on specific URLs when provided
limitations: Requires 2-5 minutes to complete extraction tasks
Consumes more credits than simple single-page scraping
Requires internet access to reach target websites
Depends on Firecrawl API availability and rate limits

use_cases.title: Competitive Pricing Analysis
use_cases.description: Extract pricing tiers and feature comparisons from competitor websites into structured JSON for analysis.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Cataloging
use_cases.description: Pull product listings with names, prices, and descriptions from online stores for inventory comparison.

use_cases.title: Directory Data Aggregation
use_cases.description: Extract business listings, contact information, and metadata from directory websites.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Data Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all pricing tiers from https://example.com/pricing and save as JSON

prompt_templates.title: Schema-Based Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract products from https://store.example.com using schema: {"type":"object","properties":{"name":{"type":"string"},"price":{"type":"number"},"category":{"type":"string"}}}

prompt_templates.title: Focused Page Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Get feature list from https://example.com/features and https://example.com/enterprise, output to features.json

prompt_templates.title: Credit-Limited Batch Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all customer reviews from https://reviews.example.com with max-credits 50, wait for completion and pretty-print output

output_examples.output: JSON file containing: tiers array with name, price, features for each plan

output_examples.input: Extract products with schema {name, price, category}
output_examples.output: JSON array of product objects with standardized fields ready for database import

best_practices: Always use --wait flag to receive results inline instead of job IDs
Provide JSON schema for predictable, structured output format
Set --max-credits limit to control spending on agent runs
Use simple scrape command for single-page extraction tasks
Review extracted JSON output before using in production workflows
anti_patterns: Running agent without --wait flag and losing track of job results
Using agent for simple single-page scraping when scrape is sufficient
Omitting schema and expecting consistent output structure
Running without credit limits on untrusted or complex websites

faq.question: How long does an extraction task take?
faq.answer: Agent tasks typically complete in 2-5 minutes depending on website complexity and number of pages to navigate.

faq.question: What is the difference between agent and scrape?
faq.answer: Agent autonomously navigates multi-page sites and figures out where data lives. Scrape extracts from a single specified page. Agent is more powerful but slower and costs more credits.

faq.question: Do I need a Firecrawl API key?
faq.answer: Yes, the firecrawl CLI requires API credentials. Ensure your environment has FIRECRAWL_API_KEY configured before using this skill.

faq.question: How do I control costs?
faq.answer: Use the --max-credits option to set a credit limit for each agent run. This caps spending for that specific extraction task.

faq.question: What format is the output?
faq.answer: Output is JSON. Use --schema for structured output matching your schema, or freeform JSON without it. Use -o filename.json to save to a file or --pretty for readable formatting.

faq.question: Can I extract from multiple URLs?
faq.answer: Yes, use --urls option to provide starting URLs. The agent will navigate from those entry points to find and extract the requested data.
